message:
perf(search): pre-size best_by_message HashMaps with known hit count

Five HashMap::new() allocations in SearchClient semantic-search paths
were rehashing mid-loop as hits streamed in. Each path already knows
the upper bound (the source Vec's len()), so switch to
HashMap::with_capacity(...) to skip at least one and usually two
power-of-two grow/rehash cycles:

  - semantic two-tier search executed:            tier_hits.len()
  - frankensearch semantic search (two-tier):     fs_hits.len()
  - ANN search path:                              ann_results.len()
  - frankensearch semantic search (single-tier):  fs_hits.len()
  - dedupe_by_message helper:                     results.len()

Correctness unchanged: de-duplication semantics are identical. The
capacity is an upper bound (we skip hits whose doc_id fails to parse
and hits with a higher distance than the incumbent), so we never
under-allocate. At typical fetch_limit (500-1000), the pre-size saves
two rehash passes per search.
